tags.qa_notes: Deep QA 2026-06-03. HALLUCINATION CORRECTED: Previous markdown said 'Permanent moorings are attached to the wreck' — WRONG. Multiple sources (DiveJourney, Sportif Dive, Carib Dive Guide) confirm NO permanent moorings; entry is paratrooper-style negative entry. Corrected in Entry/Exit and Tips sections. Casualty count: Wikipedia says 'one crewman died immediately' with 8 injured; NOW Grenada (2025) says 'two crewmen' died total; freedomtourtravel confirms '3 total deaths'. Conflict noted explicitly in markdown. Length 180m confirmed by Wikipedia. Stern loss in late 1992 confirmed by Wikipedia. Sources expanded to 5 distinct domains.

Deep QA 2026-06-03. No new factual errors found. Depth 3-8m confirmed. Created 2006 confirmed. Jason deCaires Taylor confirmed creator. Sculptures colonised by coral confirmed. WanderingWagars confirms Lene Kilde and Rene Froehlich also contributed sculptures. No changes to markdown needed.

Deep QA 2026-06-03. ADDITION: Site is named for the flamingo tongue snail (Cyphoma gibbosum), not flamingo birds — confirmed by multiple sources including Sandals blog and Royal Caribbean snorkeling guide. Flamingo tongue snails, seahorses, pipefish confirmed as site-specific species. Depth 5-18m confirmed. PADI lists as Reef and Wall. Naming detail added to markdown overview. Marine life section updated: flamingo tongue snails, seahorses, pipefish, longsnout seahorses, banded jawfish added as confirmed species.

Deep QA 2026-06-03. HALLUCINATION REMOVED from markdown: 'Eagle rays cruise the sandy transitions at the reef base' — eagle rays NOT confirmed at Boss Reef by primary sources (puregrenada.com/boss-reef, ECO Dive, Sportif Dive). None of the Boss Reef-specific sources mention eagle rays. Caribbean reef sharks also not confirmed at Boss Reef. Confirmed species: lobster, spotted moray eels, green moray eels, trunkfish, hawksbill turtles, green turtles, large crabs, banded coral shrimp, nurse sharks, stingrays, barracuda. The distinctive barracuda hole at 14-18m confirmed. Marine life section rewritten to remove unconfirmed species.

Deep QA 2026-06-03. Confirmed: tug boat wreck in shallows, slope to 28m, volcanic rock, sand channels, gorgonians, black coral. PADI and ECO Dive confirm: grouper, French angelfish, chromis, spotted moray eels, octopus, stingrays, lobster. Nurse sharks NOT confirmed at Dragon Bay by primary sources (Tips section reference removed). Octopus confirmed by multiple sources. Tip about nurse sharks under overhangs removed from Tips section. Sources updated to 4 distinct domains.

Deep QA 2026-06-03. MARINE LIFE CORRECTION: Seahorses removed from Marine Life section — not confirmed at Purple Rain specifically (seahorses confirmed at Veronica L/Flamingo Bay, not Purple Rain). Moray eels confirmed at Purple Rain (shershegoes.com). Nurse sharks added: PADI Purple Rain page lists nurse sharks as confirmed. Lobsters, stingrays, turtles, barracuda confirmed. Angelfish, queen triggerfish, grunts, trunkfish, filefish confirmed by Sportif Dive. Named after violet Creole wrasse confirmed by ECO Dive. Sources updated to 4 distinct domains.

Deep QA 2026-06-03. Length: DreamWrecks (130ft), one-million-places (40m/131ft), PADI/ECO Dive (120ft) — discrepancy noted, 130ft used as most-cited. Depth 15m confirmed by SCUBATravel and one-million-places. Sinking 1993 confirmed. Relocation to Grand Anse/Boss Reef confirmed. Marine life: seahorse, frogfish, eels confirmed by DreamWrecks. Orange cup coral confirmed. French angelfish, damselfish, sergeant majors, horse-eye jacks, barracuda, porcupine fish, creole wrasse confirmed by shershegoes.com. Sources updated to 4 distinct domains.

Deep QA 2026-06-03. Length: DreamWrecks says 180ft (54.9m); FreedomTourTravel says 160ft; ECO Dive says 180ft. 180ft most commonly cited, used in markdown. Depth: DreamWrecks 110ft (33.5m), ECO Dive 55-100ft (16-30m), caribbeanauthority.com says 32m. JSON depth set at 32m consistent with previous QA. Spiral staircase CONFIRMED by Caribbean Authority and PADI. Cement pillows CONFIRMED. Orientation upright on sand confirmed. Sinking cause (cement shift + blocked pumps) confirmed by multiple sources. Marine life: gorgonians, sea fans, encrusting sponges confirmed by DreamWrecks; green moray eels confirmed by ScubaBoard trip report. Cleaner shrimp, arrow crabs, barracudas, French angelfish confirmed by FreedomTourTravel. Sources updated to 4 distinct domains.

Deep QA 2026-06-03. HALLUCINATIONS REMOVED from markdown Marine Life section: 'Eagle rays and the occasional hammerhead are documented at the deeper reef sections' — NEITHER confirmed at Northern Exposure by any primary source. Hammerheads not confirmed at this site (no primary source lists them here). Eagle rays not confirmed at this site. Removed from markdown. Marine life rewritten: confirmed species per primary sources — porcupine fish, Spanish hogfish, yellowtail snapper, schoolmasters, creole wrasse, parrotfish, huge conch, eel garden, turtles (occasional). Note: Northern Exposure location confirmed as southwest Grenada reef, NOT north coast — ECO Dive and Sportif Dive describe it as extending from St George's harbour 5 miles SW to Point Saline. The existing markdown incorrectly describes it as 'north coast' — corrected. Sources expanded to 4 distinct domains.

Deep QA 2026-06-03. Confirmed unresolvable. 'Spice Island Reef' not found as a named site in any primary Grenada dive source. The turq.com guide mentions a 'Spice Isle Reef' (note: different spelling) described as 'sloping wall with valleys and sandy nursery area, finger and brain corals, yellowtail snapper, blue chromis, sea plumes.' This may be the same site under a different name, but coordinates and depth cannot be cross-checked. Only 1 source found (turq.com). Cannot reach 3-source validation threshold. Cannot mark validated. Turq.com description added to markdown as the only available source.

Deep QA 2026-06-03. Additional source confirmed: Sportif Dive corroborates Happy Valley as wall/drift dive. Turq.com confirms 'Valley of the Whale' as a section of Boss Reef at up to 60ft with coral hills and sand canyons (slightly different from the PADI Happy Valley description which places it at 20-90ft). ECO Dive is authoritative here, describing it as shelving slope. Current drift to Dragon Bay confirmed by PADI. Admiralty anchor confirmed. No new hallucinations found. Sources updated to 4 distinct domains.

Deep QA 2026-06-03. 'Happy Hill' not found in any primary dive source. Multiple searches return 'Happy Valley' instead. Cannot confirm this is a distinct site from Happy Valley. Previous QA found only 3 sources (ECO Dive, PADI, scuba.com) for a generic Grenada southwest reef — none specifically name 'Happy Hill'. Cannot reach 3-source validation for this specific named site. Marking unvalidated. Markdown updated to clarify no site-specific sources found.
